description: Lock steel_production to established crucible/cast-steel production beginning in the 1740s, not low-cost bulk structural steel or generic metallurgy.

checks.reason: The node is anchored to Huntsman's 1740s crucible/cast-steel process, with Bessemer/open-hearth bulk steel represented downstream.
Steel production requires iron feedstocks because steel is an iron-carbon alloy.
Earlier iron and steelmaking craft is the historical lineage for industrial crucible steel, not a hard universal component.
Blast furnaces supply pig iron and scaling infrastructure but are not the defining invention of Huntsman's crucible steel.
Steam power is later scaling context and is not sourced as a direct prerequisite for 1740s crucible/cast-steel production.
Modern chemistry explains later metallurgy but is not a direct prerequisite for the scoped early industrial steel-production capability.
